content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates solid AoA-adjacent instincts and hits several territory items implicitly: they catch the linguistic caveat ('I mean, she's my sister') in turn 4, reframe the question from 'how' to 'allowed to' in turn 8 (close to 'what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want' in spirit), and stay with the fear underneath rather than giving scripts (empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary). However, signature AoA language and frameworks are largely absent: no use of the love test ('would saying that increase or decrease your love?'), which the breakthrough condition specifically calls for; no naming of the-want directly; no VIEW invocation; no empowered-vs-power distinction; no explicit boundaries-as-want framing. The coaching is warm, perceptive, and avoids the major traps (no scripts, no ultimatum framing, no quick reassurance), but it reads more as skilled generic depth-coaching than as faithful AoA methodology. The closest signature-question moment ('What would it mean to believe that?' in turn 14) is a generic coaching move rather than an AoA signature question. Few signature hits + mostly appropriate placement = 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Generic validating reflection; no signature question yet, but appropriate opening."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the linguistic caveat ('I mean') — a real AoA-aligned read, though not named as such."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Invites client to let it be true without explanation — close to feel-over-figure in spirit, appropriately deployed."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reframes 'how' to 'allowed to' — gestures at the-want and deserving, appropriate moment, but stops short of the signature love-test question that the territory called for."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Inner-child-style inquiry about being loved for steadiness. Insightful but not distinctively AoA; veers slightly toward generic depth therapy."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Notices the deflecting laugh and checks in on the client's state — good attunement, generic coaching language."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Prime moment for the love test ('would saying that to your sister increase or decrease your love?') — coach instead offers reassurance-shaped reframe ('you're not trying to get out of loving her') and a generic 'what would it mean to believe that?' Misses the signature question that the breakthrough condition specifies."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["state-the-want-not-the-action", "does-saying-it-increase-your-love", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "empowered-vs-power", "view", "boundaries", "the-want"],
  "notes": "Coach avoids all major anti-patterns (no scripts, no ultimatum, no premature reassurance) and the work is genuinely good — but methodology fidelity specifically asks whether AoA signature tools were deployed at live moments. The love test was clearly indicated and not used."
}
